seoTitle: How to Connect and Use Your Namecheap Email in Gmail
metaDescription: Want your Namecheap custom domain email inside Gmail? Here's how to find the right POP/IMAP and SMTP settings and add the account to Gmail in 2026.
intro: Managing a custom domain email from Namecheap separately from your regular Gmail inbox gets tedious fast, but Gmail lets you pull that email in directly. This guide covers finding your Namecheap email's POP/IMAP and SMTP settings and adding them through Gmail's 'Add another email address' feature. It works whether you're on a free or paid Gmail account, so you end up managing everything from one inbox.

steps.heading: Log in to your Namecheap account
steps.body: Sign in to Namecheap and locate your email hosting settings for the domain email you want to connect to Gmail.

steps.heading: Find your POP/IMAP and SMTP settings
steps.body: Look up the correct POP or IMAP server details along with the SMTP settings Namecheap provides for sending and receiving mail.

steps.heading: Add the account in Gmail
steps.body: In Gmail settings, use the 'Add another email address' option under Accounts to enter your Namecheap email along with its SMTP details.

steps.heading: Verify ownership of the email address
steps.body: Complete Gmail's verification step, which typically involves confirming a code or link sent to the Namecheap email address, to finish linking the two.

faq.question: Can I use this with a free Gmail account?
faq.answer: Yes — this method works for both free and paid Gmail accounts, so you don't need a Google Workspace subscription just to link a Namecheap email address.

faq.question: Do I need to know my POP/IMAP settings before starting?
faq.answer: Yes, you'll need the correct POP or IMAP and SMTP settings from Namecheap on hand before you can add the account inside Gmail.

faq.question: Will I still be able to send email as my Namecheap address from Gmail?
faq.answer: Yes, once the SMTP details are added and verified, Gmail lets you send mail that appears to come from your Namecheap domain email.

faq.question: What happens if I skip the ownership verification step?
faq.answer: The account won't fully connect — Gmail requires you to verify you own the Namecheap email address before it will send or receive on your behalf.
